reasoning: As of late October 2025, just weeks before the November 16 election, polls consistently show Johannes Kaiser in fourth place. According to a Cadem survey from October 19, Jeannette Jara (29%) and José Antonio Kast (24%) are far ahead of Kaiser (13%). While Kaiser saw an earlier surge during the year, he has remained behind the main left and right candidates, and most importantly, behind Kast who captures the majority of the far-right base. Given the 16-point deficit behind the frontrunner and the short time remaining until the election, the likelihood of Kaiser surging to win the most votes in the first round is virtually zero. A 2% probability aligns perfectly with the market estimate and the realities of large polling gaps nearing election day.
